name: GitHub Copilot / Microsoft — agent memory system
context_primitives: **Copilot Memory** (preview): repository- and user-scoped **facts** with **citations to code locations**, re-verified on use so stale memories are dropped as code changes—explicitly **long-term memory across chats** (what Alex is still building). **VS Code agent memory** scopes: user (/memories/), repo (/memories/repo/), session (/memories/session/) with auto-load of first 200 lines of user memory. **Semantic repo indexing** for chat/cloud agent. Cross-feature memory (review → cloud agent).
deployment_pattern: Enable memory preview flags; curate repo memories for conventions; rely on citation refresh for accuracy; combine with semantic index for code Q&A.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: GitHub blog describes agentic memory design goals; no public LongMemEval leaderboard from Microsoft in docs.
ecosystem_integrations: GitHub.com, VS Code, Copilot CLI, OpenAI Codex agent on GitHub.
differentiators: vs. **Alex**: Microsoft ships **durable, scoped, cited memories** and **JIT verification**—ahead of Alex's admitted gap on **new-chat long-term memory**. Alex is stronger on **in-session** middle truncation for **massive trace blobs**; Copilot Memory targets **facts about code and prefs**, not multi-MB span dumps.
significance_for_builders: For **cross-session user/repo knowledge**, study Copilot Memory's citation model; for **single-session trace storms**, study Alex sub-agents + head/tail store.
key_timeline_or_milestones: 2025 GitHub blog 'Building an agentic memory system for GitHub Copilot'; VS Code memory tool preview.

primary_sources.title: GitHub Blog — agentic memory for Copilot
